w:r.w:t: Thank you for your application. Great to see that you’re interested in the developer position at Tentwenty. We’ve reviewed your CV & portfolio and they look great! Let’s proceed with the next step :) We hope to meet you soon.

Now that we have taken the first step we would like to put your development skills to the test. What sets you apart from others? This document will guide you through the steps of the developer assessment. In case of any questions, don’t hesitate to get in touch with us (email/ call).

Welcome to the Front-end Developer Technical Assessment. The goal of this task is to simulate a real-world product scenario where you will build a simplified Timesheet Management application using supplied APIs.
You are expected to demonstrate good development practices, clean code structure, and a usable, responsive UI. You may use any frontend framework (preferably React or
You are to build a small SaaS-style Timesheet Management App with the following functionality
On success, redirect to the dashboard
Using next-auth will be a plus
Display a list of timesheet entries with the following features:
Form validation and error handling for the creation form
All the API calls on the client side should have internal API routes
The code should be easy to understand, not too complex, and something that any other dev can understand.
Responsive, clean layout, and good usability
Proper async handling, error states, token usage
Proper use of hooks or libraries for state
Extra credit for tests using any framework
ReadMe with setup + notes on structure and assumptions
Submit via GitHub (public or private with access)
You do not have to over-engineer. We are assessing how you:
Developer empathy (good naming, clear separation of concerns)
We’re looking forward to your submission. Good luck with the assessment and keep on rocking!
Assessment done? Submit your work via email to us ✉️
